# SpedFind

> Pro-Grade Network Diagnostics

SpedFind is a highly accurate, free, and open-source internet speed test tool that measures download speed, upload speed, latency (ping), jitter, and packet loss.

## Project Information
- **Repository:** https://github.com/nishal21/SpedFind
- **Live Website:** https://nishal21.github.io/SpedFind/
- **Author:** Nishal

## Tech Stack
- **Frontend:** React, Vite, Tailwind CSS v4
- **Backend API:** Python, FastAPI (Streaming data for accurate speed testing)
- **Deployment:** GitHub Pages (Frontend), Render/VPS (Backend)

## Features
- **Neo-Brutalist Cyber-HUD UI:** Immersive, developer-focused interface.
- **Accurate Telemetry:** Uses `ipinfo.io` for ISP, ASN, and geographic location.
- **Network Evaluation:** Automatically grades the network (S, A, B, C, F) and provides suitability tags (e.g., 8K Streaming, Casual Gaming).
- **Data Consumption Tracking:** Measures exact payload size used during tests.
- **Real-Time Visualization:** SVG-based live area charts for download/upload monitoring.

## Application Architecture
- `/web`: React frontend using Vite and native Tailwind CSS v4.
- `/api`: FastAPI backend streaming endpoints for true download/upload measurements without using legacy speedtest APIs.
- `/core`: Shared core Python logic.
- `/cli`: Command-line interface for terminal usage via `rich` library.
